campus: Genoa (San Martino Polyclinic Hospital area / biomedical teaching sites)
degree_class: LM-9 R (Pharmaceutical, Veterinary and Medical Biotechnologies)

academic_requirements.summary: Two-year master's degree (120 CFU) delivered in English. Described on the programme page as 'free access, but requires passing a test': enrolment is gated by curricular prerequisites (minimum CFU in named subject areas) plus an assessment of adequacy of personal readiness. For the non-EU (visa-applicant) UniGeApply selection it is treated as restricted access with a remote interview/test and a minimum CGPA of 75%.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A bachelor's degree of at least 180 CFU/ECTS (or equivalent), including eligibility under degree class L-2; for the non-EU selection, a bachelor in the field of biology, microbiology, biological sciences or biotechnology is required.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Minimum curricular credits (CFU) are required across: Mathematics/Statistics 6 CFU, Informatics 3 CFU, Physics 5 CFU, Inorganic Chemistry 6 CFU, Organic Chemistry 6 CFU, General Biology/Genetics 18 CFU, Biochemistry/Molecular Biology 18 CFU, Anatomy/Physiology 9 CFU, Pathology/Microbiology 5 CFU.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Non-EU UniGeApply selection requires a minimum 75% CGPA.
academic_requirements.committee_or_pre_evaluation: An assessment of adequacy of personal readiness (free of charge) checks the curricular prerequisites; non-EU candidates undergo a remote interview/test during pre-selection on UniGeApply.

accepted_proofs: An English language proficiency certificate at B2 level, or proof that the prior degree was taught with English as the medium of instruction (for the non-EU UniGeApply selection).
exemption_rules: Proof of English-medium instruction can substitute for a B2 certificate in the non-EU selection.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: B2 English is a stated access requirement; for visa applicants the certificate is uploaded on Universitaly after acceptance.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Assessment of adequacy of personal readiness (curricular prerequisite check); for non-EU candidates a remote interview/test during UniGeApply pre-selection.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants must satisfy the curricular prerequisites and pass the readiness assessment; non-EU visa applicants additionally sit a remote interview/test.
entry_exam_or_test.format: [uncertain] The exact format (interview vs. written test vs. documentary review) of the personal-readiness assessment is not detailed on the official knowledge-assessment page; the non-EU UniGeApply page specifies a remote interview/test.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: [uncertain] Specific selection/test dates not published on the programme page beyond the application windows.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Non-EU selection requires minimum 75% CGPA and a bachelor in the biology/biotechnology field.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
First-year pre-registration/enrolment for this programme runs from 9 April to 30 November 2026 per the programme deadlines page.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Confirmation/enrolment from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026, and until 10 March 2027 with late payment.
application_deadline_eu.notes: Master's spring close is 28 August 2026 institution-wide; the programme deadlines page lists the longer pre-registration/enrolment window for residents.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
Programme-specific early closure: Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology (master, English) closed in the fall session on 13 February 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After UniGeApply eligibility, visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment (Universitaly opened 4 May 2026; University validation from 18 May 2026) and then apply for the study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The early-closure date (13 Feb 2026) is published on the UniGeApply page and matches the project's known_context. The UniGeApply fall window for A.Y. 2026/27 has closed.

required_documents.notes: Required for the UniGeApply pre-evaluation/selection of this English master.
Bachelor's degree (min 180 CFU) in the biology/microbiology/biotechnology field.
Used to verify the curricular prerequisites (minimum CFU per subject area) and the 75% CGPA for non-EU candidates.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate (B2) or proof of English-medium instruction
required_documents.notes: B2 English is an access requirement; certificate uploaded on Universitaly post-acceptance for visa applicants.
Passport mandatory for non-EU applicants on UniGeApply; residence permit for non-EU spring-session residents.

required_documents.document_name: Legalization or CIMEA Statement of Verification, plus Declaration of Value or CIMEA Statement of Comparability
required_documents.notes: Non-EU candidates with a foreign degree submit the foreign qualification with Italian translation and Declaration of Value (or CIMEA equivalents) before enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment submission and study-visa evidence
required_documents.notes: Visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment after UniGeApply eligibility, then apply for the study visa.

source_quotes.quote: MEDICAL-PHARMACEUTICAL BIOTECHNOLOGY, Corso di laurea magistrale, Teaching Language English, Degree Class LM-9 R, 120 CFU, Genova
This degree course is free access, but requires passing a test.

a Bachelor degree (of at least 180 CFU/ECTS); minimum CFU by area: Mathematics/Statistics 6, Informatics 3, Physics 5, Inorganic Chemistry 6, Organic Chemistry 6, General Biology/Genetics 18, Biochemistry/Molecular Biology 18, Anatomy/Physiology 9, Pathology/Microbiology 5; B2 English level
Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnologies: required bachelors in the field of biology, microbiology, biological sciences, biotechnology; minimum CGPA 75%; remote interview/test during pre-selection
Medical Pharmaceutical Biotechnology (master's, English): early closure 13 February 2026 (fall session)

uncertainty_notes: Restricted access confirmed: the programme is 'free access but requires passing a test' (curricular prerequisites + personal-readiness assessment), and is treated as restricted with a remote interview/test for the non-EU UniGeApply selection.
The exact format of the personal-readiness assessment (interview vs. written test vs. documentary review) is not detailed on the official knowledge-assessment page, which returned navigation only.
The known fall early closure of 13 Feb 2026 is confirmed on the UniGeApply page.

official_call_url points to the English-master non-EU pre-evaluation selection page; the canonical selection page slug for A.Y. 2026/27 resolved to unige.it/en/internazionale/iscrizioni-internazionali/unigeapply-international-admissions-selection-ay-202627 in one fetch.
